file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16- AMENDMENT #2 TO THE DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned has read this Amendment, agrees hereto, and is an authorized representative of its respective party. INGRAM MICRO INC. NETGEAR, INC. 1600 East St. Andrew Place 4401 Great America Parkway Santa Ana, CA 92705 P.O. Box 58185 Santa Clara, CA 95052-8185 By: /S/ AC Mann By: /s/ PATRICK Lo ---------------- -------------------- Name: AC Mann Name: PATRICK Lo Title: VP Purchasing Title: V. P. Netgear, Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned has read this Amendment, agrees hereto, and is an authorized representative of its respective party. Section 2, TERRITORY, is amended to be United States, [*]. INGRAM MICRO INC. NETGEAR, INC. 1600 East St. Andrew Place 4401 Great America Parkway Santa Ana, CA 92705 P.O. Box 58185 Santa Clara, CA 95052-8185 By: /S/ AC Mann By: /s/ PATRICK Lo ---------------- -------------------- Name: AC Mann Name: PATRICK Lo Title: VP Purchasing Title: V. P. Netgear, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the territory amendment into the signature block creates ambiguity. Contract terms, especially key terms like territory, should not be embedded within the signature block as it leads to confusion about whether the parties agreed to those specific terms. Territory definition is a material term and putting it where it doesn't belong makes it difficult to enforce.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-204 states that a contract for the sale of goods can be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement, including conduct by both parties which recognizes the existence of such a contract. Burying a critical amendment like the territory within the signature section obscures clear agreement, potentially leading to disputes over whether both parties genuinely intended to include that term. The placement affects clear demonstration of agreement and could be challenged as not clearly part of the agreement. The signature section has been specifically changed to include language related to territory, therefore the change can have severe ramifications.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-204. Formation in General. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-204. Formation in General. (1) A contract for sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement , including conduct by both parties which recognizes the existence of such a contract . (2) An agreement sufficient to constitute a contract for sale may be found even though the moment of its making is undetermined. (3) Even though one or more terms are left open a contract for sale does not fail for indefiniteness if the parties have intended to make a contract and there is a reasonably certain basis for giving an appropriate remedy. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a critical term (territory amendment) into the signature block. This placement obscures clear agreement, potentially leading to disputes over whether both parties genuinely intended to include that term. This contradicts UCC § 2-204, which states that a contract for the sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement. The snippet states, "A contract for sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement, including conduct by both parties which recognizes the existence of such a contract." Placing the amendment in the signature block undermines a clear showing of agreement.
